title: Active Critical Alert: VM experiencing CPU contention - Virtual Machine
cause: The issue can be caused due to ESXi host CPU overcommitted (high CPU contention/ready time), VM configured with more vCPUs than required (CPU scheduling delays), CPU-intensive applications running inside the VM, other VMs on the same host consuming excessive CPU, and CPU limits or reservations configured incorrectly.
resolution: The issue can be resolved with migrating the VM to a less utilized ESXi host (vMotion), right-sizing the VM by reducing unnecessary vCPUs, optimizing or stopping high CPU-consuming processes in the guest OS, balancing workload across hosts or adding CPU capacity to the cluster, and reviewing and adjusting CPU reservations, limits, and shares.
